item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
they were led to believe there was a person or object at SCP-1409 of great value,

I think that was meant to be, "object at Point 1409-Alpha"

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: Oh neat!
item.content:encoded:
Is this related to the 'lonely whale', a unique call of one whale heard repeatedly?
I'd assume so based on it - it seems like a really neat way of creating a skip around the phenomena. I'd feather this in as a really nice short scip with a good premise and delivery, but I feel like there's more that could be done with it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope this whale is related to the one that cause whales to beach themselves with its song.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded, "a period of time" is much to vague for these types of documents. At the least something like, "Individuals showing symptoms have thus far required a minimum of 10 minutes exposure time."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty cool, and I knew that the whale behind the song would be the REAL problem long before I reached the addendum.
Imagine what would happen if "Are We Cool Yet?" broadcast this in a major city. That will give terror to anyone who thinks this article has none.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this a shoe before? Either way. I like the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A minor complaint here: Megaptera Novaeangliae should be Megaptera novaeangliae as per convention. (The species name is always lowercase.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's been fixed. Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One quibble, the very last sentence doesn't sound right, "legislative" is defintely not the right word. How about this instead? "The theoretical humpback whale which created SCP-1409 is provisionally designated SCP-1409-A."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They did. It resulted in half a sheet of music notes scribbled out and crumbled up in the corner. He also destroyed a tuba and somehow warped a saxophone around a clarinet as if trying to strangle one with the other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. I like the premise, and I like what you did with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Affected individuals will not, however, be able to recreate the song themselves, claiming any attempt to do so, or even to hum the tune, sounds ‘wrong’.

So they haven't tried exposing the SCP to a musician capable of writing sheet music?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you can't say it fairer than that, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing's good, but the concept doesn't really grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obviously, MTF Theta-5 is stationed aboard SCPS Pequod. Don't see the issue. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some typos in this, and it made me think of the SCP shoes that make people march into the ocean.

That said, I like two things: that point Alpha is described, as well as what is found there (nothing), and that it's actually the theoretical whale (which you may want to designate SCP-1409-1) that's the SCP and they're still looking for it. I was thinking precisely that as I started reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The worst thing about this is how much sense it makes. Gosh darn it, Reject, you've just managed to ruin one of my own creations for me within 6 hours of posting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd change "If an individual listens to SCP-1409 for a certain period of time" to something a bit more specific. Something like "If an individual listens to the full duration of SCP-1409".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit.

That's what I was thinking too though to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the source.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tentatively upvoted. Though I actually think the Foundation _might_ train affected people to find it, because — if they're better at locating the source than the Foundation is — the Foundation can follow them there, then scoop up the source as a side benefit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the critique. I've fixed the first point by making it clear they only bother when it involves Foundation workers and people who are important, and saying that people who directly study the recording have to know how to dive as a precaution. Fixed the second by removing the line about that point.

You got me on the Pequod, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not the hell your whale SCP.

Okay, upvoted because there are enough things here I like to counteract the things I'm ehhh on. First, why is the Foundation bothering to spend time and effort extensively training these people and equipping them to make the trip? Is it just kindness, keeping them from dying and possibly breaching security? Second, it's hard for me to believe that the SCP is an irresistable compulsion that you can temporarily talk someone out of. It's like postponable insanity; you have an incurable disease that forces you to carry out a series of behaviors, unless someone offers to teach you scuba diving. Third, the MTF should have been the SCPS Pequod and you know it.
